nodes.title: Platform: iOS-only for v1
nodes.rationale: Solo dev, fastest path to ship, smallest surface area to maintain.

nodes.title: Sync model: CloudKit vs custom backend vs local-only
nodes.prompt: How should sessions sync across devices?

options.label: CloudKit
options.body: Free, Apple-only, zero backend ops. Locks in iOS-only forever.

options.label: Custom backend
options.body: More work now, but keeps the door open to Android/web later.

options.label: Local-only (defer)
options.body: Ship without sync. Revisit in v2 once we know whether users care.

nodes.title: Notifications: local-only
nodes.rationale: No backend means no push infrastructure. Local notifications cover the v1 use case (end-of-session alert).

nodes.title: iOS background timer limits
nodes.mitigation: Schedule a local notification at the deadline so the user is notified even if the in-app timer is killed by the OS.

nodes.title: Apple Watch parity expectation
nodes.mitigation: TBD. Could position v1 as iPhone-first explicitly in store copy, or accept the gap.

nodes.title: Streaks: first-class for v1 or stretch goal?
nodes.prompt: Should streaks ship in v1 or be deferred?

options.label: First-class for v1
options.body: Strong retention hook. Adds per-day session tracking, gap handling, recovery UI.

options.label: Stretch goal
options.body: Ship without; revisit if engagement metrics suggest it would help.
